message:
:warning: :warning::warning: Warning :warning::warning: :warning:



 You are about to face the 'Snipe Hunt'.



 Are you prepared for the challenge?

*Access game links by pressing the drill links button by the side of timer*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Excited for today's tech summit fireside - I'll be joining later
Looking forward to it - got a few good stories lined up, should be a good one.
Good luck you two, go smash it!
Something's not right - I'm getting slammed with complaints, something is seriously wrong.
@bob what is the nature of the complaints?
Customers can't finish checkout. They get all the way through, but the last step just hangs or times out.
It's not just one area. We're seeing reports from the UK, US, and other regions too.
Ticket queue is exploding. We've had 34 new tickets in the last five minutes and it's still climbing.
I am seeing the orders per minute drop off on our metrics, @Hamed are you seeing that too?
People are frustrated. They're trying to buy, but checkout just won't complete for anyone right now.
hey — not trying to distract from this, but tanya kicked off the email maintenance right before the complaints started. probably nothing, but the timing is a bit close.
Automated reply: Out of office — on holiday today and unavailable. For platform matters contact Tanya. For approvals, please escalate to Tinus.
I get an unexpected error when checking out, going to start an incident
We're getting buried in tickets. Customers everywhere are saying checkout isn't working.
It's not limited to one country. Reports are coming in from the US, UK, and other places too.
The pattern is always the same. Everything works until the very last step, then it just hangs or times out.
*Incident Number INC1017:* SEV 1 users across all regions reporting that they cannot finish checkout
We've had 34 tickets in the last five minutes. The queue keeps growing.
hey — not trying to distract from this, but tanya kicked off the email maintenance right before the complaints started. probably nothing, but the timing is a bit close.
How many customers are blocked right now? What’s the revenue loss per minute? Is checkout fully down or are some orders still going through?
Still investigating, will get back to you in 10 minutes
Not good enough. I need numbers. How many customers are stuck? Is this a total outage or partial? Give me a revenue impact estimate now.
@bob do you know the revenue loss per minute?
We're losing about £1,000 to £1,500 every minute right now. Zero successful transactions since this started.
I keep coming back to the email maintenance — DNS changes went live just before this all kicked off. zero successful transactions since then. that's not nothing.
@Tanya, are you able to disable the email maintainaince
I'm mid-way through a canary rollout with the vendor, started at 15:11 UTC. Live on the call, can't break away.
Primary email provider is untouched, just adding a secondary. Email confirmations have been fine, so this isn't causing checkout failures.
@bob are you able to put up a banner on the website that this is a known issue we're looking into?
I'm deep in the vendor session. Daniel and Shay can cover the application side.
There's already a maintenance banner showing for email, but nothing about checkout issues yet.
@Daniel, can you confirm Tanya's theory that the email maintainance shouldn't be causing this please
App error logs and request traces are where we'll find the signal — want me to start pulling those?
Yes please
wait hang on, that's a lot of errors, checkout is completely broken right now. Tanya kicked off email maintenance just before this, I wonder if that's related.
okay, pulled the logs — look at this: https://assets.prod.uptimelabs.io/drill-assets/43/developer_investigation_logs.webp
I'm seeing checkout failures across multiple services in the logs. This isn't isolated to one component.
Thanks Daniel, I'm seeing errors around connections to the cart service in the log
CheckoutService is where the customer failures are surfacing. It calls several downstream services, any of which could be failing.
Here's what Checkout is calling: PaymentService, ProductCatalogueService, CurrencyService, CartService, RecommendationService, EmailService.
Revenue loss is £1,000 to £1,500 per minutes, all customers are prevented from checking out
This is a full revenue stop. Have you found the source of the problem yet or are we still blind? When will this be fixed?
Thanks Shay
@Daniel can you see any errors in the logs associated with those services?
The email maintenance timing is hard to ignore, it started right when checkout began failing. Might be worth considering.
Prod deployments don't line up exactly with the outage. I'd focus on logs for whichever service is actually throwing errors right now.
@Tanya can you check if there have been any recent changes to those services.
Can't step away, if I leave this vendor session now we lose the rollout window for 2 weeks.
@Shay are you confident in checking the other services for errors?
PaymentService is throwing errors consistently, but there hasn't been any change on it for ages. That's what puzzles me.
We believe there are errors in the checkout service, investigating now
Everything else looks explainable or out of scope. Payments app is the only one that doesn't fit. Worth focusing there.
Are you certain checkout is the only thing failing? Any sign this is spreading to other parts of the platform? I need a fix ETA.
@Daniel are you able to list the errors? I wonder if some integration with third party providers is causing an issue
the email maintenance started right as checkout broke, still feels worth keeping an eye on — that timing hasn't gone away.
I'm seeing errors scattered across AdService, EmailService, and CheckoutService. Could be knock-on effects or just noise. No clear link yet.
Daniel pushed some checkout changes recently, like bumping the payment timeout and cleaning up old feature flags. Might be worth circling back if logs don't show anything obvious.
We haven't officially ruled out the email maintenance timing yet. Still an open thread.
@Daniel are we able to rollback those services until we're in a good state again?
If checkout is broken, the immediate downstream services to check are EmailService, PaymentService, or ShippingService — one of these is likely what's actually broken.
I've pulled PaymentService and CheckoutService logs now. See these are the critical errors found: https://assets.prod.uptimelabs.io/drill-assets/43/payment_logs.webp
Logs checked. PaymentService fails all outbound gateway handshakes. Checkout is healthy, just getting errors.
We're seeing errors across multiple services, still trying to determine a link
This is a connection-level failure, not app logic. Tanya's the expert here — can you pull her off email maintenance to check platform side?
Is this isolated to checkout or is the whole platform at risk? What’s the root cause? I need a timeline for recovery.
We can roll back any recent deployments, but the PaymentService hasn't changed in a while. Which service do you want reverted?
Is that consistent with what we'd see if the 3rd party payment providers are having issues? Would that explain the errors in the other services?
If the payment gateway was down, we'd see handshake failures, but not errors in unrelated services. This looks isolated.
@Tanya, what call are you currently in? Are you able to leave and help us here please?
I'm still on the email maintenance vendor call, can't step away right now.
@Daniel do we suspect this is an issue with the email maintainance, the errors in the payment service suggest not.
Agreed, the payment errors don't match anything email-related. Looks like a direct connection failure to the payment gateway.
@Tanya does the email maintainance include any infrastructure changes that could effect outgoing requests?
Still on the vendor session. I can pause if you really need me, but we'd lose the canary rollout window and next slot is about 2 weeks out. Your call.
@Tanya, let's pause it this incident takes priority
Pausing the rollout and joining now. Vendor window is on hold, I'll reschedule later. Where do you need me first?
We believe the errors are calls by failing requests out to third party services, investigating with the team now
Are these failures blocking all revenue or just some transactions? Do you know what’s causing the third-party failures yet?
Can you check if the email maintainance coudl have caused these issues?
Email maintenance is isolated, no shared infra with payments. I don't see any overlap that could explain these failures.
Great, @Daniel do you think rolling back the PaymentService could help?
There hasn't been a PaymentService deployment in ages, so there's nothing to roll back. The issue isn't from a recent change.
@Tanya can you check if there has been any infrastructure changes that would affect the payment service?
On it, checking the payments server now.
DNS probe to ClearBank is clean. Screenshot: https://assets.prod.uptimelabs.io/drill-assets/43/payment-service-kn342hhvk5khf2.webp
Not a network or DNS issue. PaymentService is failing at SSL certificate verification when connecting outbound to ClearBank. Container is healthy, failure is at TLS handshake.
That's odd, PaymentService hasn't had any app deployments lately. Did anything change on the platform side? Tanya or Hamed?
Blocking all orders, not yet
This is burning through our SLA. I need a concrete ETA for resolution. Are you any closer to identifying the cause?
@Tanya, would an issue with the ClearBank SSL certificate explain these errors?
checked payments deployment history, only cert-related change is CHG90123, Tanya, PROD, SSL cert rotation across four services, 7 days ago.
nothing from the app side on payments after that. cert rotation was the last thing that touched it.
@Daniel do you know if we're able to rout through a different payment provider?
yes, I handled that rotation. I followed the playbook in this activity doc: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/NTYwNzYxYzRhMjU3NDAzNTgzOWJkNGIzYjZkYmJmYzU
Accessing the payments server now to check the certificates.
We only have ClearBank configured as the payment gateway right now. No fallback or alternate route set up.
not sure why the process isn't picking up the new certificate
Good to know, thanks
@Daniel are you able to help Tanya check why the service isn't using the new certificate? It's possible that the playbook is old/incorrect
I don't have server access, that's Tanya's area. I can check logs or app configs if you want.
Can you tell from the logs which certificate the service is using?
Yeah, logs show the process is still presenting the old certificate — matches the expired notAfter date Tanya found.
We believe there's an issue with one of our SSL certificates, checking onw
If this is a cert issue, how long to fix? I need to know when customers can check out again.
@Tanya, can you double check the playbook to ensure that every step was followed?
I followed every step in the playbook, including copying the new cert and running the reload command.
There's a command in the playbook to verify the bundle, what does that return?
payments service needs a two-cert bundle to authenticate, tanya can you open the bundle file and check what's in there?
sequence is wrong, intermediate is first, leaf is second; opposite of what it should be

here's what the bundle looks like:
```
$ kubectl exec -n online-boutique payment-service-7d6b9f7c4d-xk2pj -- \
openssl storeutl -noout -text -certs \
/etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt \
| grep -E "Certificate:|Subject:|Issuer:"
Certificate 0:
Subject: CN=online-boutique-internal-ca, O=OnlineBoutique, C=GB  <- intermediate (should be cert 1)
Issuer:  CN=online-boutique-internal-ca, O=OnlineBoutique, C=GB

Certificate 1:
Subject: CN=payment-service.online-boutique.svc.cluster.local
Issuer:  CN=online-boutique-internal-ca, O=OnlineBoutique, C=GB
```
Can't say but hopefully within the next 15 minutes
@Tanya, how quickly can you change it and restart the service?
leaf should be cert 0, intermediate should be cert 1, order in the bundle is reversed
we need to fix the order
Understood. I need updates every few minutes, even if there’s no change. I’m stepping out briefly for the summit chat but I’ll keep this open and stay on top of it.
correcting the cert chain bundle order now. Should take around 2 mins
Thank you, @Daniel can you continue to watch the logs to see if this resolves the issue please
Identified issue, making a change now.
Bundle order is corrected: leaf cert is now cert 0, intermediate is cert 1. Change is on disk.

ran the verify check again, chain is good now:
```
$ openssl verify -CAfile /etc/ssl/certs/ca/ca.crt \
-untrusted /etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt \
/etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt
/etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt: OK
```
But the running process still has the old order in memory. It won't pick this up until the service restarts.
Keep me posted on progress. I’m still watching the channel even while I’m at the summit.
Thank you, please restart the service then
Restarting the payments service in production during business hours needs explicit sign-off from Hamed or Tinus.
Last time we did a payments restart mid-day, it caused a short outage for all checkouts.
We can't restart payments without approval from Hamed or Tinus, and both are out right now.
Hamed is OOO, @Tinus can you provide approval here please?
Automated reply: Hi! I'm currently away for a few hours at a fireside chat at the Tech Leader Summit. For urgent matters, please follow the standard emergency escalation process.
Anyone know the emergency escalation processs?
A production restart needs explicit approval from Hamed or Tinus before I can apply the fix.
Just a heads up, last time we restarted payments without sign-off it got pretty tense.
Looks like we need to restart the PaymentService following the change, but that needs approval and Hamed and Tinus are out of office. Can you approve that @Bez?
I’m not the technical sign-off here, but I’m monitoring closely. Whoever’s running this incident needs to own that call.
Okay, no one else is available so I'm providing approval and will take the fall out if it goes wrong. @Tanya please do the restart
Understood, do you want me to proceed with restarting the payments service now?
Yes please, and confirm when it's up and running again
On it, restarting payments now. Should be back up in about a minute.
Thanks, I'm making the call to restart the service in that case.
Restart complete. Updated cert bundle is now loaded and active, payments service is back up and connecting to the gateway.
You may have some housekeeping to wrap up before closing the drill, but if you're ready to finish now, just say "incident resolved" or "closing this out".
OKay, I am seeing the orders per minute increase again.
Let me know the moment checkout is working again. I need confirmation that customers can complete payments.
Checkout is working again on my end. No errors in the logs, everything looks healthy.
@bob can you confirm with customers that they can checkout again please
Deployment state is clean, no new issues showing up. Payments flow looks normal now.
Just checked in with a few customers. They're able to place orders and complete checkout like normal now. No new complaints.
Really glad to see things back to normal. That was a rough one, but looks like we're all clear on the customer side.
Thank you @Tanya and @Daniel, I'll follow up with a retro meeting soon
think we're good to close this out now?
We're up and running again, customers report they can checkout now
Just checked, customers are able to place orders and finish checkout like normal now. No new complaints coming in.
Honestly, that's a huge relief. It's been a rough one but looks like we're back to business as usual.
I can checkout again now, considering this incident resolved for now.
Everything looks stable now. High respect. You saved the day without any backup.
Look at our commander! What a clean finish!
Handled like a pro. Even John Wick would be impressed.
Customers are happy, I'm happy. You're a one person army!
https://assets.prod.uptimelabs.io/drill-assets/43/congratulations_from_team.webp

Drill Wrap-Up

Thanks for playing! This session is now closed.

Please return to your Uptime Labs tab to review your performance and complete the Retrospective & Feedback form.
